title: Melicope tietaensis
intro: Melicope tietaensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the tietaensis lemon myrtle. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Melicope tietaensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The leaves and bark of this plant are commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
